issues.title: [Epic · Week 1] Product, data, legal, and UI discovery gate
issues.outcome: Complete the product, data, legal, and UI discovery gate without pulling later platform work into the current gate.
scope: Own the phase dependency order.
Surface risks and scope cuts.
Close the phase only with evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: Every child issue has a clear owner, milestone, and acceptance criteria.
Blocked work is represented using issue dependencies.
The phase gate has explicit evidence.
definitionOfDone: Acceptance criteria are verified.
Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.

issues.title: [Week 1 · Product] Project charter, community promise, and production workflow
issues.outcome: Approve and publish a concise project charter plus a low-overhead, human-accountable workflow that lets one maintainer and AI collaborators deliver a trustworthy static Arcaea wiki MVP.
scope: Create `docs/product/PROJECT_CHARTER.md` defining the mission, target users, product promise, project values, decision priorities, and the relationship between passion project, community product, and portfolio evidence.

Create or expand `CONTRIBUTING.md` with supported contribution types, contributor expectations, issue-first planning, local setup, branch naming, commit guidance, pull-request requirements, review flow, and evidence expectations.
Document the community promise for factual accuracy, source provenance, respectful corrections, accessibility, privacy, security, licensed assets, and timely takedown or correction handling.

Define responsibility boundaries for the maintainer, human contributors, and AI collaborators; AI may propose, implement, test, and review, but final product, legal, licensing, release, and merge decisions remain human-owned.

Define the Definition of Ready used before implementation starts, including an explicit outcome, scoped tasks, non-goals, dependencies, owner, affected surfaces, legal or data constraints, test plan, and required evidence.

Define the Definition of Done used before merge or closure, including acceptance verification, appropriate automated and manual checks, documentation, provenance, accessibility, security, performance, and exception records.

Define the production workflow from issue triage through branch creation, implementation, self-review, pull request, checks, human approval, squash or merge decision, release evidence, and issue closure.
Define how scope changes, blocked work, rejected alternatives, risk acceptance, and known exceptions are recorded without relying on private chat history.
Link the approved charter and contribution workflow from `README.md` and align them with `docs/product/WEB_MVP_BRIEF.md` and `docs/roadmap/WEB_MVP_ROADMAP.md`.
nonGoals: Do not redesign the MVP, reopen the wiki-first product decision, or introduce runtime viewer features.
Do not add a backend, account system, moderation platform, Kubernetes, or other infrastructure solely to make the workflow appear production-grade.
Do not create a complete legal opinion or claim endorsement by lowiro or the official Arcaea project.
Do not require enterprise-scale governance, multiple mandatory reviewers, semantic-release automation, or processes that a solo maintainer cannot sustain.
Do not allow AI-generated text, code, data, or review comments to bypass human accountability or source and licensing checks.
technicalConstraints: The workflow must remain practical for approximately one maintainer working about three focused hours per day during the six-week MVP roadmap.
Repository files and GitHub issues or pull requests are the canonical record; decisions required to reproduce or review work must not exist only in ChatGPT, Codex, Discord, or local notes.

Documentation must preserve the GPL-3.0-only repository license and clearly separate repository licensing from permissions for game facts, names, prose, images, audio, charts, and other third-party assets.
The fan-project statement must be unambiguous: Arcaea-Viewer is unofficial, community-oriented, not endorsed by lowiro, and must provide a visible correction or takedown path.
No workflow step may require committing secrets, proprietary game files, unlicensed assets, personal data, or hidden credentials as evidence.
Use existing repository checks and GitHub capabilities where sufficient; new tooling must solve a demonstrated gap and remain maintainable.
acceptanceCriteria: `docs/product/PROJECT_CHARTER.md` states the mission, primary audience, MVP promise, project values, decision hierarchy, governance owner, and unofficial fan-project position in language understandable without reading source code.

The charter explicitly prioritizes usefulness to ordinary Arcaea players, sustainable community contribution, and credible portfolio evidence without allowing portfolio goals to override product, legal, accessibility, security, or maintenance needs.
`CONTRIBUTING.md` documents at least documentation or data corrections, UI or accessibility changes, and code changes, with a clear path from issue selection to accepted pull request.
Branch, commit, pull-request, review, merge, evidence, and issue-closing expectations are specific enough that a contributor or AI coding agent can follow them without inventing missing policy.

A reusable Definition of Ready checklist covers outcome, scope, non-goals, dependencies, ownership, affected files or surfaces, legal and provenance needs, acceptance criteria, tests, and required evidence.

A reusable Definition of Done checklist covers implemented scope, verified acceptance criteria, relevant automated checks, manual review, documentation, source or asset provenance, accessibility, security, performance, and recorded exceptions.

The workflow identifies decisions that always require the maintainer's explicit approval, including product scope, legal or licensing interpretation, publication of third-party assets, security exceptions, release readiness, and merge authority.

The community promise defines how corrections are proposed, how disputed facts are sourced, how contributors are credited, how unsafe or unlicensed submissions are rejected, and how takedown requests are handled.

`README.md`, the charter, contribution guide, product brief, and roadmap do not contradict each other about the wiki-first MVP, static delivery, excluded runtime features, or production-quality meaning.
The resulting process avoids unnecessary infrastructure and can be demonstrated with one documentation or content correction and one small code change.
testPlan: Perform a documentation consistency review against `README.md`, `docs/product/WEB_MVP_BRIEF.md`, `docs/roadmap/WEB_MVP_ROADMAP.md`, issue #3, issue #11, and issue #12; record and resolve every contradiction found.

Walk through the documented workflow using two tabletop scenarios: a factual metadata correction with a source citation and a small UI or accessibility code change. Confirm that each scenario reaches a clear Ready and Done decision.
Verify that every documented command, path, template, and repository link is valid from a clean checkout or clearly marked as future work.

Run repository checks appropriate to the changed files. If code, templates, or automation are changed, run `pnpm check`; for documentation-only changes, run available Markdown, link, formatting, or roadmap validation checks.

Run `./scripts/bootstrap-roadmap.sh --dry-run --phase week-1 --repo Dyu20705/arcaea-viewer` and confirm the managed issue content matches the manifest with no unexpected issue, label, milestone, parent, or dependency changes.
Have the maintainer complete the final human review for fan-project wording, community commitments, licensing boundaries, and AI responsibility boundaries.
evidence: Links to the merged charter, contribution guide, README updates, and any reusable issue or pull-request templates created by this work.
A pull-request checklist mapping each acceptance criterion to the file or section that satisfies it.
Recorded results of the two tabletop workflow scenarios, including any ambiguity found and the documentation change that resolved it.
Output or summary of applicable automated checks and the roadmap bootstrap dry-run.
An explicit maintainer approval comment covering the unofficial fan-project statement, community promise, licensing boundaries, AI collaborator policy, and final production workflow.
definitionOfDone: All acceptance criteria are checked and linked to reviewable repository evidence.
The charter, contribution guide, and README links are merged and are consistent with the product brief, legal or provenance guardrails, and six-week roadmap.
The Definition of Ready and Definition of Done are reusable by later roadmap issues rather than being specific only to issue #2.
The documented workflow has been successfully exercised by both tabletop scenarios and no unresolved high-impact ambiguity remains.
Applicable checks pass, and the roadmap dry-run shows no unexpected reconciliation changes.
Every legal, accessibility, security, privacy, performance, or maintenance exception is either resolved or explicitly recorded with an owner and follow-up issue.
The maintainer has provided the required human approval evidence and the issue can be closed without relying on private conversation context.

issues.title: [Week 1 · Product] Wiki-first PRD, MVP boundaries, sitemap, and user journeys
issues.outcome: Replace the viewer-first MVP definition with a wiki-first product definition for ordinary Arcaea players.
scope: Define personas and primary tasks.
Approve routes and content taxonomy.
Define homepage, explore, song, pack, partner, story, game-topic, information, settings, legal, and error surfaces.
Define explicit cuts and post-MVP path.
acceptanceCriteria: PRD matches docs/product/WEB_MVP_BRIEF.md.
URL query parameters own sort/filter state.
Runtime, upload, playback, analytics, and replay are excluded from the MVP navigation.
Primary user journeys are testable.
definitionOfDone: Acceptance criteria are verified.
Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.

issues.title: [Week 1 · UX] Research references and approve an original Arcaea-inspired UI direction
issues.outcome: Produce an original desktop-first UI direction based on broad reference research rather than copying Sekai Viewer or a wiki.
scope: Research at least eight relevant sites.
Document information architecture, navigation, cards/tables, filters, image treatment, and accessibility findings.
Create wireframes and a high-fidelity prototype for home, explore, and song detail.
Test Hikari-inspired light and Tairitsu-inspired dark token candidates.
acceptanceCriteria: Accepted UI direction includes rejected alternatives and reasons.
Both themes pass preliminary contrast review.
Component inventory and state matrix exist.
Design notes include responsive, performance, and reduced-motion implications.
definitionOfDone: Acceptance criteria are verified.
Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.

issues.title: [Week 1 · Data] Approve static versioned catalog architecture and content access contract
issues.outcome: Choose a static, versioned JSON/data-module architecture that supports wiki pages without a backend.
scope: Define catalog boundaries and IDs.
Define build-time validation and generated indexes.
Define browser loading, caching, stale-version, and failure behavior.
Record triggers that would justify a backend later.
acceptanceCriteria: ADR selects static versioned data for MVP.
No backend is added.
Contracts support songs, charts, packs, partners, story, game topics, sources, and assets.
Future migration path does not leak infrastructure into current code.
definitionOfDone: Acceptance criteria are verified.
Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.

issues.title: [Week 1 · Legal] Fan-project, provenance, source, and licensed-asset guardrails
issues.outcome: Define what facts, prose, links, and media may be published and how permission evidence is retained.
scope: Adopt the official-first source hierarchy.
Define facts-only use of unlicensed sources.
Require source and asset records.
Define takedown and correction contact path.
Define forbidden repository content.
acceptanceCriteria: Every content entity can reference source records.
Every published image has permission evidence or an accepted open license.
Community wiki prose and assets are not copied without compatible permission.
Public disclaimer and takedown process are specified.
definitionOfDone: Acceptance criteria are verified.
Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.

issues.title: [Week 1 · Planning] Canonical six-week execution order, dependencies, and scope cuts
issues.outcome: Make the issue hierarchy and six-week gate order the single execution source of truth.
scope: Map all existing issues to current MVP, deferred, superseded, or post-MVP.
Document capacity and weekly gates.
Add dependency relationships and sub-issues.
Document what to cut first if schedule slips.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/roadmap/WEB_MVP_ROADMAP.md is canonical.
No active MVP issue depends on runtime features.
Every issue has phase, priority, area, milestone, and ownership labels.
Superseded issues explain their replacement.
definitionOfDone: Acceptance criteria are verified.
Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.

issues.title: [Week 1 · Data] Define metadata schemas, provenance model, and legal seed catalog
issues.outcome: Define the normalized records and create a small internally consistent seed catalog for product development.
scope: Schemas for songs, charts, packs, partners, story entries, game topics, sources, assets, and releases.
Stable IDs and cross-entity references.
Small seed catalog with permitted placeholders/assets.
Validation rules and content-size limits.
acceptanceCriteria: Schemas reject missing IDs, broken references, invalid URLs, duplicate keys, and unreviewed assets.
Seed data exercises every MVP page template.
Each record has game-version and review metadata.
No protected chart file or audio is required.
definitionOfDone: Acceptance criteria are verified.
Relevant documentation and tests are updated.
No unreviewed legal, accessibility, security, or performance exception is introduced.
